__WBJEE__

__West Bengal Joint Entrance Examination __

About the exam\-

![](data:image/png;base64,)

- The West Bengal Joint Entrance Examinations Board administers the West Bengal Joint Entrance Examination, also known as the WBJEE, which is a state\-controlled, centralised test for admission to numerous private and public engineering institutes in West Bengal\. 
- After completing grade 12, students take the test to be admitted to graduation \(also known as bachelor's\) courses\. Anyone who completed the physics, chemistry, and mathematics courses in the pure science and vocational 10\+2 streams at the plus two level is eligible to sit the exam\.

Overview/Highlights of this exam\-

__Particulars__

__Details__

Name of the examination

West Bengal Joint Entrance Examination

Exam conducting authority

West Bengal Joint Entrance Examination Board

Frequency of the exam

Once a year

Courses offered through the exam

Admission to undergraduate Engineering Colleges of West Bengal

Examination level 

State level

Medium of examination 

English and Bengali

Mode of the examination

pen\-paper based

Exam official website

wbjeeb\.in/wbjee

Eligibility criteria for this exam\-

![](data:image/png;base64,)

NATIONALITY

- The candidate needs to be an Indian national\.

AGE REQUIREMENTS

- To sit for the exam, a candidate must be at least 17 years old \(as of December 31, 2022\) or have been born on or before that day\. There is no maximum age for taking the exam\.
- However, as of December 31, 2022, the minimum age requirement for enrollment to the Marine Engineering Course is 17 years old, and the maximum age requirement is 25 years old\.

Paper pattern & marking scheme of the examination \-

__Particulars__

__Details__

Mode

Offline \(pen\-and\-paper\-based test\)

Duration 

4 hours \(2 hours for each paper\)

Medium of Exam

English and Bengali

Sections

Paper 1 \- Mathematics

Paper 2 \- Physics and Chemistry

Type of Questions

Multiple Choice Questions

Total Number of Questions

155 Questions

![](data:image/png;base64,)

- Category I: One mark will be given for each accurate response\. A wrong answer results in a deduction of one\-fourth of a mark\. Any combination of responses that include more than one option will be deemed wrong and receive a score of \-1/4 \(25% negative\)\.
- Category II: A correct response earns two marks but only one option is acceptable\. A wrong answer will receive \-1/2 \(25% negative\) marks\. Any combination of responses that include more than one option will be deemed wrong and receive a score of \-1/2 \(25 percent negative\)\.
- Category III: A few selections might be accurate\. Only accurate answers will earn you two \(two\) marks\. There are no failing grades\.

Important events & their dates related to exam\-

![](data:image/png;base64,)

__Events__

__Dates__

Registration open for the exam

December 

Last date for registration

January

Admit card releasing date

April 

Exam date

April

Online declaration of answer key

June

Results of the exam

Exam Application process \-

Register for the WBJEE online in 2022\. Candidates must first complete the WBJEE registration process for 2022 by following the instructions provided: 

 STEP 1 \- Visit the WBJEE 2022 official website at wbjee\.nic\.in\.

STEP 2 \- Select the link for "New Registration\."

STEP 3\- Enter information such as your name, your parents' names, your birthdate, your gender, the sort of identification you have, and your phone number\.

STEP 4 \- additional contact information \(address, district, state, PIN code, email ID, mobile number, etc\.\)

 STEP 5 \- With the response, create a password and security question\.

STEP 6\- Click "Submit" after reviewing the information you gave during WBJEE registration\.

And selection procedure of the exam\-

- Candidates will be chosen based on how well they did on the entrance exam\. Students who pass the exam should register for online counselling sessions and pay the counselling cost at Allahabad bank using an e\-challan\.
